title: silene rubella subsp. bergiana
intro: Silene rubella subsp. bergiana, also known as red campion, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting respiratory health. In herbal preparations, red campion is often used as a tea or tincture, and it can also be dried and used in infusions or combined with other herbs for its medicinal properties.
